title: HTML-in-Canvas引爆前端！AI时代互联网视觉效果完全不一样了

fetch_attempts.note: Full text looks sufficient for claim extraction.

events: The article states that HTML-in-Canvas is currently an experimental feature and has entered the W3C/WICG system as a proposal.
The article states that a similar HTML-in-Canvas idea was proposed in 2016, was shelved by browser vendors at the time, and has recently been revived and advanced by Google.
The article states that Pretext bypasses the browser's DOM layout system, uses pure mathematical calculation for text layout, and is claimed to be hundreds of times faster than traditional methods.
The article states that Pretext became very popular, with the author's post receiving tens of millions of views and its GitHub repository quickly gaining more than 40,000 stars.
techniques: The article states that HTML-in-Canvas is a method that renders HTML by putting it into Canvas.
The article states that after adding the layoutsubtree attribute to a canvas tag, developers can call drawElementImage through a 2D canvas context to draw child elements into the canvas.
tools: The article states that in Chrome, users can enable the HTML-in-Canvas experimental feature through the flag at chrome://flags/#canvas-draw-element.
The article states that Pretext is an open-source frontend tool created by Midjourney engineer Cheng Lou.
